justifications.completeness: The extraction misses the majority of objects (reports 1 pedestrian vs 4 ground-truth and 2 vehicles vs 5), so coverage is very low.
justifications.semantic_richness: When objects are reported the model provides several useful attributes (actions, context, motion status, door/trunk states, age), but many entities are entirely missing and some context categories are not filled.
justifications.spatial_relations: The extraction includes some plausible relations (e.g., a truck parked on the left and a nearby pedestrian, one vehicle moving), but relations are sparse, partially ambiguous, and not mapped to most ground-truth objects.
justifications.overall: Overall performance is limited by major omissions of objects despite reasonably detailed attributes for the few detected entities, yielding low-to-moderate utility.

justifications.completeness: Detected only one of four pedestrians and two of five vehicles, missing most objects and many ground truth annotations.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides object types, motion states, and some spatial phrases but omits most pedestrian actions and detailed contextual labels from the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations for the reported entities are reasonable, but many objects lack any spatial relation and there are inconsistencies with motion status.
justifications.overall: Partial and somewhat accurate descriptions for a few objects are outweighed by low coverage and several incorrect or missing attributes, resulting in a low overall performance.

justifications.completeness: Missed both pedestrians and reported only four of five ground truth vehicles, so the extraction is largely incomplete.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides many per-vehicle attributes (type, motion_status, trunk_open, doors_open, spatial_relations) but omits all pedestrian attributes and contains some mislabels, yielding moderate richness.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes several spatial relations for vehicles which add value, but relations are sometimes vague or not clearly aligned with the ground truth and pedestrians have none, so accuracy is partial.
justifications.overall: Useful semantic fields are present but critical misses (two pedestrians missing), some misclassifications, and a missed vehicle reduce the overall quality to below average.

justifications.completeness: Detected only one of two pedestrians and two of four ground-truth vehicles, so about half of expected entities are present.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides many per-object attributes (age, motion, door/trunk status, spatial hints) beyond the ground truth, but some contextual labels are coarse or slightly incorrect.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes explicit spatial relations but some are inaccurate—e.g., claiming the pedestrian is between the truck and building contradicts the ground truth that they walk along the side of the road—and relations are missing for several vehicles.
justifications.overall: Moderate overall: good semantic detail but incomplete detection and some incorrect spatial claims limit reliability.
